notes: MCP-zone interface contract rules for mcp-bea-rpp. These 16 rules are STRUCTURAL INVARIANTS of the tool responses, not SQL queries against an Iceberg table, so they are NOT executed by `python -m brightsmith.infra.dq_runner`. They are verified in two places: (1) the 65-case eval set at `data/ai_ready/eval/mcp-bea-rpp-eval.jsonl` executed against the live `FutureProofMCPServer` — data-analyst reports 65/65 PASS per `governance/eda/mcp-bea-rpp-eda.md`; and (2) the 70-function pytest suite at `tests/mcp/test_get_regional_price_parity.py` (42 tests) and `tests/mcp/test_compare_purchasing_power.py` (28 tests). Data-quality of the underlying Gold table is covered by the 55 rules in `governance/dq-rules/gold-regional-price-parities.json`; this MCP-layer file adds only the wire-level contract guarantees that the Gold rules cannot see. Every rule below is ALREADY verified — this file's job is to make those guarantees explicit, auditable, and regression-proof.

rules.name: data_source field present with valid enum value
rules.description: Every success response from get_regional_price_parity MUST include a top-level `data.data_source` field whose value is IN ('bea_official', 'estimate'). This closes Bronze Condition 7 at the wire level — Gemma MUST be able to hedge numeric precision when the underlying value is an estimate.
rules.threshold: 100% of success responses have data.data_source IN ('bea_official','estimate')
rules.rationale: Bronze staff review condition 7 (spec lines 15-19, 220-226). EDA section 'Key Findings #2' (governance/eda/mcp-bea-rpp-eda.md lines 40-43) confirms all 8 BEA rows match Gold byte-for-byte across 12 fields including data_source. EDA 'Tool Response Verification' (lines 160-178) confirms byte-for-byte match for data_source across all 16 cases above. Also asserted by tests/mcp/test_get_regional_price_parity.py response-shape tests.

rules.name: governance.quality_tier equals partial_verification
rules.description: Every success response from either MCP tool MUST include a `governance.quality_tier` field equal to the literal string 'partial_verification'. This communicates to Gemma that only 8 of 51 states are BEA-verified and the remaining 43 are estimates.
rules.rationale: Spec acceptance criterion (line 238): 'governance.quality_tier attached to every response is partial_verification'. Because this field is framework-controlled by `BaseMCPServer.attach_governance`, the EDA (lines 319-326) notes eval cases do NOT assert it — so this rule's verification is performed by the pytest suite (tests/mcp/test_get_regional_price_parity.py `test_governance_tier_*`) rather than the eval set. Included in `eval_case_ids` above as the in-process probe vehicle; the tests are the ground truth.

rules.name: bea_official rows are exactly the 8-state canonical set
rules.description: Every success response where `data.data_source == 'bea_official'` MUST have `data.state_fips` IN the canonical 8-state set {'05' (AR), '06' (CA), '11' (DC), '15' (HI), '19' (IA), '28' (MS), '34' (NJ), '40' (OK)}. No other state can be BEA-verified; no BEA-verified state can be missing.
rules.threshold: |{state_fips where data_source='bea_official'}| == 8 AND set equality with {'05','06','11','15','19','28','34','40'}
rules.rationale: Spec line 233: 'All 8 BEA-verified states return correct cost_tier + adjusted_Nk matching the Gold spot-check table.' EDA 'Strict-mode exhaustive sweep (51/51)' (lines 180-188) probed every one of the 51 Gold rows and confirmed exactly the 8-state `bea_official` set: AR, CA, DC, HI, IA, MS, NJ, OK. Zero cross-contamination. The 16 eval cases above (8 verified-* full-payload + 8 strict-ok-* strict-mode pass) exercise every member of this set.

rules.name: adjusted_examples has exactly 4 keys: 30k, 50k, 75k, 100k
rules.description: Every `get_regional_price_parity` success response MUST have a `data.adjusted_examples` object whose key set is EXACTLY {'30k', '50k', '75k', '100k'} — no more, no fewer, no renames.
rules.threshold: 100% of get_regional_price_parity success responses have set(data.adjusted_examples.keys()) == {'30k','50k','75k','100k'}
rules.rationale: Spec response schema (lines 63-69) pins the 4-key structure. EDA 'Tool Response Verification' (lines 160-178) confirms all 8 BEA rows match Gold byte-for-byte across `adjusted_30k`, `adjusted_50k`, `adjusted_75k`, `adjusted_100k`, and the MCP handler (spec line 173) builds the struct client-side from these 4 columns. Tested structurally in tests/mcp/test_get_regional_price_parity.py.

rules.name: purchasing_power_multiplier returned at full precision
rules.description: The `data.purchasing_power_multiplier` field in every success response MUST be returned at FULL precision (not rounded to 4 decimals). Verifiable by checking that `round(salary * ppm, 2)` reproduces the Gold `adjusted_Nk` values EXACTLY for at least the 8 BEA-verified states at all four salary levels.
rules.threshold: For every bea_official case: round(30000*ppm,2)==adjusted_examples['30k'] AND round(50000*ppm,2)==adjusted_examples['50k'] AND round(75000*ppm,2)==adjusted_examples['75k'] AND round(100000*ppm,2)==adjusted_examples['100k']
rules.rationale: Pre-review Advisory #1 (cited in EDA line 58) required full-precision ppm so the caller can reconstruct the arithmetic. EDA lines 58-61 confirm the eval rows carry `0.9033423667570009` for CA and `1.1389521640091116` for IA, matching Gold. EDA 'Tool Response Verification' table (lines 170-178) lists the full-precision ppm for all 8 BEA rows. If ppm were rounded to 4 decimals (e.g., 0.9033), the $100K CA computation would produce 90330.00 instead of the spec-required 90334.24 — this rule guards against that regression.

rules.name: cost_tier is a valid enum value
rules.description: Every success response's `cost_tier` (top-level in get_regional_price_parity, nested under state_a/state_b in compare_purchasing_power) MUST be IN the 5-value enum ('very_high', 'high', 'average', 'low', 'very_low').
rules.threshold: 100% of success responses have cost_tier IN ('very_high','high','average','low','very_low')
rules.rationale: Gold contract (governance/data-contracts/consumable-regional-price-parities.yaml) declares cost_tier as a 5-value enum. Gold DQ rule GRP-VAL-004 enforces this at the Iceberg layer; this MCP rule enforces it on the wire, protecting against a future bug where the MCP handler accidentally normalizes, translates, or nulls the field. EDA confirms byte-for-byte match on cost_tier for all 8 BEA verified states (EDA lines 170-178, cost_tier column).

rules.name: Strict mode returns data for all 8 BEA-verified states
rules.description: `verified_only=true` with a `state_fips` in the 8-verified set MUST return a success response (`data` is NOT null) with `data_source='bea_official'`. Strict mode MUST NEVER false-negative against a BEA-verified state.
rules.threshold: 8 of 8 strict-ok-* cases return data != null AND data.data_source == 'bea_official'
rules.rationale: Spec acceptance criterion (line 234): 'Strict mode returns all 8 verified states successfully.' EDA 'Strict-mode exhaustive sweep' (lines 180-188) confirmed 8/8 bea_official rows succeed under strict mode with zero cross-contamination. The eval's 8 `strict-ok-*` cases cover every verified state by FIPS identity.

rules.name: Strict mode refuses estimate states with null + 'strict' message
rules.description: `verified_only=true` with a `state_fips` NOT in the 8-verified set MUST return a null `data` response whose `message` contains 'strict' (case-insensitive substring). Strict mode MUST NEVER false-positive on an estimate state.
rules.threshold: 5 of 5 strict-refuse-* cases return data == null AND message contains 'strict' AND message contains the state name
rules.rationale: Spec acceptance criterion (line 232): 'Strict mode refuses all 43 estimated states with a structured null response.' EDA 'Strict-mode exhaustive sweep' (lines 180-188) probed all 43 estimate rows and confirmed every one refuses with the canonical message 'Regional price parity for <state_name> is currently an estimate (data_source=estimate) and strict mode is enabled.' The 5 eval cases are a representative sample; the remaining 38 refusals are covered exhaustively by the pytest suite's parameterized strict-mode tests in tests/mcp/test_get_regional_price_parity.py.

rules.name: compare_purchasing_power strict mode refuses any non-verified state
rules.description: `compare_purchasing_power` with `verified_only=true` and at least one state (state_a or state_b) NOT in the 8-verified set MUST return null `data` with a message that (a) contains 'Strict mode' and (b) names the offending estimate state.
rules.threshold: 4 of 4 compare-strict-refuse-* cases return data == null AND message contains 'Strict mode' AND message identifies the offending state
rules.rationale: Spec null-case response (line 167): 'Strict mode requires both states to be BEA-official; Texas is currently an estimate.' EDA section 6 (Key Findings, lines 66-71) confirms no tool raises on mixed provenance. The 4 eval cases cover: offender in position a (TX/CA), position b (CA/TX), both verified on one side only (NJ/NY), and the canonical verified-vs-estimate (IA/FL). EDA 'Recommendations #5' (lines 290-296) notes the state_b-only offender case and the both-estimate case are handled by the implementation but pinned by tests rather than eval.

rules.name: Unknown state returns null + helpful message; never raises
rules.description: Any `state` input that does not resolve to a US state (50 + DC) MUST return a null `data` response with a `message` that contains 'Unknown state' (or equivalent helpful text) and the offending input. The tool MUST NEVER raise an exception.
rules.threshold: 7 of 7 unknown-* and compare-unknown-* cases return data == null AND no exception raised AND message is non-empty string
rules.rationale: Spec acceptance criterion (line 235): 'Unknown state returns null with a helpful error message, never raises.' EDA 'Key Findings #6' (lines 67-71) confirms every edge case probed (None, bool, int, float, list, NaN, inf, missing keys, stripped-zero FIPS, out-of-range FIPS like 99/03/00) returned a structured null response. Eval covers 5 unknown-* cases (Xanadu, Puerto Rico, PR USPS, empty, whitespace) + 2 compare-unknown-* cases (Atlantis in positions A and B). Additional defense-in-depth edges (int, float, list, None, tab whitespace, DC lowercase) are covered by the pytest suite per EDA 'Untested Input Shapes' (lines 233-272).

rules.name: Invalid salary returns null data; never raises
rules.description: Invalid `salary` input (negative, zero, > 1e7, non-numeric string, None, NaN, inf, bool) MUST return null `data` with a message referencing salary validation. The tool MUST NEVER raise. Bool is explicitly covered because Python's `isinstance(True, int)` is True.
rules.threshold: 7 of 7 compare-bad-salary-* cases return data == null AND no exception raised AND message mentions salary/positive/less than
rules.rationale: Spec null-case response (line 165): 'salary must be a positive number less than 10,000,000; got -5'. EDA 'Key Findings #6' (line 69) confirms NaN, inf, None, bool all handled correctly on the live server — no exceptions raised. Eval covers 7 salary edge cases. EDA 'Recommendations #2 and #3' (lines 281-289) flag NaN/inf/False as defense-in-depth edges covered by tests/mcp/test_compare_purchasing_power.py but not the eval set; this rule acknowledges that split.

rules.name: Same state in both positions returns null + 'must be different'
rules.description: `compare_purchasing_power` with state_a and state_b resolving to the SAME state (after normalization, not raw string) MUST return null `data` with a message containing 'must be different'. Same-state detection MUST happen AFTER state normalization so `CA` vs `California` vs `06` all get rejected.
rules.threshold: 3 of 3 compare-same-* cases return data == null AND message contains 'must be different'
rules.rationale: Spec null-case response (line 166): 'state_a and state_b must be different states; got CA and CA'. EDA 'Coverage #10' commentary (lines 141-146) highlights the normalization-vs-raw-string design point: 'same-state detection happens AFTER normalization, not on raw strings.' The 3 eval cases cover (a) identical USPS, (b) USPS vs full-name equivalence, (c) FIPS vs USPS equivalence. Whitespace equivalence (`CA` vs `  CA  `) is an EDA-flagged defense-in-depth edge (line 288, Recommendation #4) pinned by tests, not the eval.

rules.name: Canonical spec example: CA vs IA at $65K reproduces exactly
rules.description: `compare_purchasing_power(salary=65000, state_a='CA', state_b='IA')` MUST return EXACTLY `state_a.adjusted_salary = 58717.25`, `state_b.adjusted_salary = 74031.89`, `difference = 15314.64`, and `difference_pct = 26.08`. This is THE canonical spec example (line 234) and THE regression floor for the compare arithmetic.
rules.threshold: 3 of 3 cases (USPS input, full-name input, FIPS input) return the exact values above
rules.rationale: Spec acceptance criterion (line 234): 'compare_purchasing_power math matches round(salary x ppm, 2) exactly for CA vs IA at $65K -> $58,717.25 vs $74,031.89 (pre-computed in the spec's success example).' EDA 'compare_purchasing_power: arithmetic reproduction' table (lines 195-209) shows this exact line item reproducing across all 3 input-form variants. If this rule ever fails, either the Gold ppm values drifted or the MCP handler's rounding semantics broke — both are catastrophic.

rules.name: Ratio invariance: adjusted_Nk / Nk == ppm within 1e-9
rules.description: For every `get_regional_price_parity` success response, `adjusted_30k / 30000.0`, `adjusted_50k / 50000.0`, `adjusted_75k / 75000.0`, and `adjusted_100k / 100000.0` MUST all equal `purchasing_power_multiplier` within tolerance 1e-2 (the rounding noise floor from the 2-decimal `round(salary*ppm,2)` in the adjusted_Nk columns). More strictly, `abs(round(30000*ppm,2)/30000 - round(100000*ppm,2)/100000) < 1e-4` should hold as an independent invariant check on the 4-struct.
rules.threshold: For every bea_official case: max(adjusted_Nk/Nk for Nk in [30k,50k,75k,100k]) - min(...) < 1e-4
rules.rationale: Ratio invariance is why the EDA (lines 213-216) observes `difference_pct = 26.08` is stable across all 5 salary levels: the ratio `ppm_b/ppm_a - 1` is invariant under multiplication by salary. This rule pins the algebraic consequence so a future bug where adjusted_Nk values drift independently of ppm (e.g., a columnar corruption or a write-side rounding inconsistency) is caught. Tolerance 1e-4 accounts for the round-to-2-decimals in the stored adjusted_Nk columns. EDA table lines 170-178 provide the ground-truth values for the 8 BEA rows.

rules.name: Eval set has >= 50 cases
rules.description: The eval set at `data/ai_ready/eval/mcp-bea-rpp-eval.jsonl` MUST contain at least 50 cases. This is the Brightsmith MCP minimum and the spec's explicit acceptance criterion (line 236).
rules.rationale: Spec acceptance criterion (line 236): 'Eval set has >= 50 cases, all passing against the live MCP server.' EDA (line 7) reports the eval set contains 65 cases — 30% above the minimum — and (line 228) all 65 pass. This rule protects against a future trim where eval cases are accidentally removed.

rules.name: Eval set covers all 11 spec requirement categories
rules.description: The eval set MUST include at least 1 case for each of the 11 spec eval-set categories (spec lines 189-200): (1) all 8 BEA-verified states, (2) >=5 estimated states, (3) all 3 input forms, (4) case-insensitive, (5) strict-mode positive, (6) strict-mode refusal, (7) unknown state, (8) compare_purchasing_power success at common salary levels, (9) compare salary validation, (10) compare same-state rejection, (11) compare strict-mode mixed-provenance refusal.
rules.threshold: Every one of the 11 spec categories has >= 1 eval case (current count: 65 cases, all 11 categories covered at or above minimum per EDA table)
rules.rationale: EDA 'Coverage of Spec's 11 Eval Categories' table (lines 123-139) verifies exhaustively: category 1 (8/8), category 2 (8/>=5), category 3 (3/3), category 4 (5/>=1), category 5 (8/>=1), category 6 (5/>=1), category 7 (5/>=1), category 8 (7/4), category 9 (7/4), category 10 (3/>=1), category 11 (4/>=1). Every category meets or exceeds the spec minimum. The 11 eval_case_ids above are the canonical anchor case per category. Protects against a regression where the eval file is restructured and drops an entire category.
